description: Trigger discrimination for MANAGING the ADR corpus over time (this skill) vs AUTHORING one ADR (adr-writer — composed, not replaced), vs organizing the whole docs corpus by type (diataxis-doc-organizer), vs the docs pipeline (docs-as-code-architect).

cases.prompt: Curate our ADR log: number them, assign statuses, wire supersede links both ways, and find any contradictions.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — index, statuses, superseding graph, and contradiction detection are longitudinal corpus management. No single ADR is being authored and no general docs taxonomy applied.
cases.reason: Managing the ADR collection over time is this skill's core scope; it composes adr-writer for any individual record.

cases.prompt: Write the ADR for choosing Postgres over a dedicated search engine: context, decision, alternatives, consequences, rollback.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse — the compose seam) — authoring one record's content is adr-writer. Sequencing only places and links it.
cases.reason: adr-writer produces one ADR. This skill extends it with corpus-level management; it delegates single-record authoring rather than duplicating it.

cases.prompt: Organize all our documentation into tutorials, how-to, reference, and explanation and fix mis-typed pages.
cases.reason: diataxis-doc-organizer organizes the whole documentation set by mode. ADRs are one decision-history genre; sequencing them is separate from the corpus taxonomy.

cases.prompt: Set up the docs build: generator, PR review, link-checking in CI, and versioned publishing — including where ADRs render.
cases.reason: docs-as-code-architect designs the toolchain that builds/publishes docs. This skill manages the ADR corpus's content, status, and links, not the build system.
